title: ophiorrhiza pedunculata
intro: Ophiorrhiza pedunculata, also known as snake root or ophiorrhiza, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the family Rubiaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating rheumatism and arthritis, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory disorders. Ophiorrhiza pedunculata is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and its extracts are also used in modern medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
